mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.64% provides room for easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS (employment growth) over a 2-month period signals robust labor demand and consumer purchasing power expansion, driving positive equity returns as corporate earnings expectations improve over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth rate exceeds 0.2% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive employment signal, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining RSAFS (retail sales) combined with rising UNRATE signals demand contraction and labor market deterioration, reducing corporate revenue expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp within same month
regime_hint: demand shock or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative demand-labor signal, or interaction effect (RSAFS × UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in INDPRO (industrial production) over 2 months signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, constraining corporate profitability and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST (housing starts) combined with declining CPIAUCSL inflation signals demand expansion without inflationary pressure, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the Fed maintains accommodative policy.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 2% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1487K units, CPIAUCSL at 327.460
regime_hint: soft landing scenario with demand growth and disinflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive housing-inflation signal, or interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE (unemployment rate) above 4.8% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings recession risk increases.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.4%, below trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when UNRATE > 4.8%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS level (> 4.5%) combined with declining GDPC1 growth signals monetary tightening during economic slowdown, creating stagflation risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as real rates remain restrictive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% AND GDPC1 quarterly growth rate < 1.5% annualized
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with growth deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite tight monetary-slow growth signal, or interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in RSAFS (retail sales) at > 0.3% month-over-month combined with rising PAYEMS signals robust consumer demand and employment expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate earnings guidance improves.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.3% month-over-month AND PAYEMS growth > 0.15% month-over-month in same month
regime_hint: strong demand cycle with synchronized consumer and labor growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive demand-employment signal, or interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
